hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty"); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qiE "rm[[:space:]]+-rf[[:space:]]+/|DROP[[:space:]]+(TABLE|DATABASE)|TRUNCATE([[:space:]]+TABLE)?|git[[:space:]]+push.*--force.*(main|master)|git[[:space:]]+reset[[:space:]]+--hard|:\(\)[[:space:]]*\{[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*\|[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*&[[:space:]]*\}[[:space:]]*;[[:space:]]*:|chmod[[:space:]]+-R[[:space:]]+777|curl.*\|[[:space:]]*sh|wget.*\|[[:space:]]*sh"; then echo "{\"decision\": \"block\", \"reason\": \"Destructive command blocked (rm -rf /, DROP/TRUNCATE, force-push to main, git reset --hard, fork bomb, chmod -R 777, curl|sh). Use a safe alternative or run it yourself after review.\"}"; fi' || true

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty"); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qiE "git[[:space:]]+commit|git[[:space:]]+(-[A-Za-z]+[[:space:]]+)*push|git[[:space:]]+merge|git[[:space:]]+cherry-pick|git[[:space:]]+revert|git[[:space:]]+tag|gh[[:space:]]+pr[[:space:]]+(create|merge|ready)|gh[[:space:]]+release[[:space:]]+(create|edit|delete)"; then echo "{\"decision\": \"block\", \"reason\": \"Commits, pushes, tags, merges and PRs belong to the human — the sole author. Do NOT run git commit/push/merge/cherry-pick/revert/tag or gh pr/release. Instead, print the exact command(s) in a copy/paste block for the human to run.\"}"; fi' || true

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty"); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qiE "co-authored-by:[[:space:]]*(claude|anthropic)|noreply@anthropic\.com|generated with[[:space:]]*(\[)?claude code|🤖[[:space:]]*generated|claude\.com/claude-code|claude\.ai/code|generated by claude"; then echo "{\"decision\": \"block\", \"reason\": \"AI-attribution trace detected in the command (commit message, PR body, etc.). The human is the sole author — never add Co-Authored-By: Claude/Anthropic, noreply@anthropic.com, or Generated-with-Claude-Code lines to commits or PRs.\"}"; fi' || true

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty"); if echo "$FILE" | grep -qiE "(^|/)\.env$|(^|/)\.env\.|credentials|secrets?|key\.json|token\.json|\.pem$|\.p12$|id_rsa|password|private.*key" && ! echo "$FILE" | grep -qiE "\.example$|\.sample$|settings\.json$"; then echo "{\"decision\": \"block\", \"reason\": \"Refusing to write a file that may hold secrets. Use a .env.example with placeholders and keep real secrets out of the repo.\"}"; fi' || true

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.content // .tool_input.new_string // empty"); if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qiE "(sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}|gsk_[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}|AKIA[A-Z0-9]{16}|ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}|xox[bpsa]-[a-zA-Z0-9-]+|-----BEGIN[[:space:]].*PRIVATE KEY)"; then echo "{\"decision\": \"block\", \"reason\": \"Detected a probable secret/API key in the file content (OpenAI/Groq/AWS/GitHub/Slack token or private key). Load it from an environment variable instead.\"}"; fi' || true

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty"); if echo "$FILE" | grep -qiE "\.py$" && [ -f "$FILE" ]; then ROOT=$(git -C "$(dirname "$FILE")" rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd); RUFF=""; for c in "$ROOT/backend/.venv/bin/ruff" ruff; do if command -v "$c" >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ -x "$c" ]; then RUFF="$c"; break; fi; done; if [ -n "$RUFF" ]; then "$RUFF" format "$FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; "$RUFF" check --fix "$FILE" 2>&1 | tail -5 || true; fi; fi' || true

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty"); if echo "$FILE" | grep -qiE "\.(ts|tsx)$" && [ -f "$FILE" ]; then ROOT=$(git -C "$(dirname "$FILE")" rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd); if [ -f "$ROOT/frontend/package.json" ]; then (cd "$ROOT/frontend" && npx --no-install prettier --write "$FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; npx --no-install eslint --fix "$FILE" 2>&1 | head -20 || true); fi; fi' || true

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty"); CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.content // .tool_input.new_string // empty"); ISSUES=""; if echo "$FILE" | grep -qiE "\.py$"; then if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE "(^|[^.\w])print\("; then ISSUES="$ISSUES
  print( detected — use logging instead of print in app code"; fi; if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE "#[[:space:]]*type:[[:space:]]*ignore"; then ISSUES="$ISSUES
  # type: ignore detected — fix the type instead of suppressing"; fi; fi; if echo "$FILE" | grep -qiE "\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$"; then if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE "console\.(log|debug)"; then ISSUES="$ISSUES
  console.log/debug detected — remove before commit"; fi; if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE "@ts-ignore|@ts-expect-error"; then ISSUES="$ISSUES
  TS suppression detected — fix the type error instead"; fi; if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE "[^a-zA-Z_]any[^a-zA-Z0-9_]"; then ISSUES="$ISSUES
  Possible any type — prefer a specific type / unknown + narrowing"; fi; fi; if [ -n "$ISSUES" ]; then echo -e "Code quality warnings:$ISSUES"; fi' || true

bash -c 'echo "=== NOTES-TURBO SESSION ==="; echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo N/A)"; echo "Last commit: $(git log --oneline -1 2>/dev/null || echo N/A)"; echo "Pending: $(git status --short 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d " ") files"; echo "Python: $(python3 --version 2>/dev/null | cut -d" " -f2 || echo NOT INSTALLED)"; echo "Node: $(node -v 2>/dev/null || echo NOT INSTALLED)"; echo "Docker: $(docker --version 2>/dev/null | cut -d" " -f3 | tr -d "," || echo NOT RUNNING)"; echo "==========================="; if [ -f package.json ] && [ ! -d node_modules ]; then echo "Root node_modules missing. Run: npm install (activates husky)."; fi; if [ -d backend ] && [ ! -d backend/.venv ]; then echo "backend/.venv missing. Create it and install requirements-dev.txt."; fi' || true
